name: Anticipate
description: Maintain a state of informed preparedness for adversity.

name: Prevent or Avoid
description: Preclude the successful execution of an attack or the realization of adverse conditions.

name: Adaptive Response
description: Implement agile cyber courses of action to manage risks.

name: Dynamic Reconfiguration
description: Make changes to individual systems, system elements, components, or sets of cyber resources to change functionality or behavior without interrupting service.

name: Withstand
description: Continue essential mission or business functions despite adversity.

name: Prepare
description: Maintain a set of realistic courses of action that address predicted or anticipated adversity.

name: Recover
description: Restore mission or business functions during and after adversity.

name: Adapt
description: Modify mission or business functions and/or supporting capabilities to predicted changes in the technical, operational, or threat environments.

name: Continue
description: Maximize the duration and viability of essential mission or business functions during adversity.

name: Constrain
description: Limit damage from adversity.

name: Reconstitute
description: Restore as much mission or business functionality as possible after adversity.

name: Understand
description: Maintain useful representations of mission and business dependencies and the status of resources with respect to possible adversity.

name: Coordinated Protection
description: Ensure that protection mechanisms operate in a coordinated and effective manner.

name: Self-Challenge
description: Affect mission/business processes or system elements adversely in a controlled manner, to validate the effectiveness of protections and to enable proactive response and improvement.

name: Transform
description: Modify mission or business functions and supporting processes to handle adversity and address environmental changes more effectively.

name: Re-Architect
description: Modify architectures to handle adversity and address environmental changes more effectively.

name: Deception
description: Mislead, confuse, hide critical assets from, or expose covertly tainted assets to, the adversary.

name: Obfuscation
description: Hide, transform, or otherwise obfuscate information from the adversary.

name: Disinformation
description: Provide deliberately misleading information to adversaries.

name: Misdirection
description: Maintain deception resources or environments and direct adversary activities there.

name: Tainting
description: Embed covert capabilities in resources.

name: Diversity
description: Use heterogeneity to minimize common mode failures, particularly attacks exploiting common vulnerabilities.

name: Architectural Diversity
description: Use multiple sets of technical standards, different technologies, and different architectural patterns.

name: Design Diversity
description: Use different designs to meet the same requirements or provide equivalent functionality.

name: Synthetic Diversity
description: Transform implementations of software to produce a variety of instances.

name: Information Diversity
description: Provide information from different sources or transform information in different ways.

name: Path Diversity
description: Provide multiple independent paths for command, control, and communications.

name: Dynamic Resource Allocation
description: Change the allocation of resources to tasks or functions without terminating critical functions or processes.

name: Supply Chain Diversity
description: Use multiple independent supply chains for critical components.

name: Dynamic Positioning
description: Distribute and dynamically relocate functionality or system resources.

name: Functional Relocation of Sensors
description: Relocate sensors, or reallocate responsibility for specific sensing tasks, to look for indicators of adverse events.

name: Functional Relocation of Cyber Resources
description: Change the location of cyber resources that provide functionality or information, either by moving the assets or by transferring functional responsibility.

name: Asset Mobility
description: Securely move physical resources.

name: Fragmentation
description: Fragment information and distribute it across multiple components.

name: Distributed Functionality
description: Decompose a function or application into smaller functions and distribute those functions across multiple components.

name: Contextual Awareness
description: Construct and maintain current representations of the posture of missions or business functions considering cyber events and cyber courses of action.

name: Dynamic Resource Awareness
description: Maintain current information about resources, status of resources, and resource connectivity.

name: Dynamic Threat Awareness
description: Maintain current information about threat actors, indicators, and potential, predicted, and observed adverse events.

name: Mission Dependency and Status Visualization
description: Maintain current information about the status of missions or business functions, dependencies on resources, and the status of those resources with respect to threats.

name: Non-Persistence
description: Generate and retain resources as needed or for a limited time.

name: Non-Persistent Information
description: Refresh information periodically, or generate information on demand, and delete it when no longer needed.

name: Adaptive Management
description: Change how mechanisms are used based on changes in the operational environment as well as changes in the threat environment.

name: Non-Persistent Services
description: Refresh services periodically, or generate services on demand and terminate services when no longer needed.

name: Non-Persistent Connectivity
description: Establish connections on demand, and terminate connections when no longer needed.

name: Privilege Restriction
description: Restrict privileges based on attributes of users and system elements as well as on environmental factors.

name: Trust-Based Privilege Management
description: Define, assign, and maintain privileges associated with active entities, based on established trust criteria, consistent with principles of least privilege.

name: Attribute-Based Usage Restriction
description: Define, assign, maintain, and apply usage restrictions on systems containing cyber resources based on the criticality of missions or business functions and other attributes (e.g., data sensitivity).

name: Dynamic Privileges
description: Elevate or decrease privileges assigned to a user, process, or service based on transient or contextual factors.

name: Realignment
description: Align system resources with core aspects of organizational missions or business functions.

name: Purposing
description: Ensure systems containing cyber resources are used consistent with critical mission or business function purposes.

name: Offloading
description: Offload supportive but non-essential functions to other systems or to an external provider that is better able to support the functions.

name: Restriction
description: Remove or disable unneeded functionality or connectivity, or add mechanisms to reduce the chance of vulnerability or failure.

name: Replacement
description: Replace low-assurance or poorly understood implementations with more trustworthy implementations.

name: Specialization
description: Modify the design of, augment, or configure critical cyber resources uniquely for the mission or business function to improve trustworthiness.

name: Analytic Monitoring
description: Monitor and analyze a wide range of properties and behaviors on an ongoing basis and in a coordinated way.

name: Monitoring and Damage Assessment
description: Monitor and analyze behavior and characteristics of components and resources to look for indicators of adversary activity, and to detect and assess damage from adversity.

name: Redundancy
description: Provide multiple protected instances of critical resources.

name: Protected Backup and Restore
description: Back up information and software (including configuration data and virtualized resources) in a way that protects its confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity, and enable restoration in case of disruption or corruption.

name: Surplus Capacity
description: Maintain extra capacity for information storage, processing, or communications.

name: Replication
description: Duplicate hardware, information, backups, or functionality in multiple locations and keep them synchronized.

name: Segmentation
description: Define and separate system elements based on criticality and trustworthiness.

name: Predefined Segmentation
description: Define enclaves, segments, or other types of resource sets based on criticality and trustworthiness, so that they can be protected separately and, if necessary, isolated.

name: Dynamic Segmentation and Isolation
description: Change the configuration of enclaves or protected segments, or isolate resources, while minimizing operational disruption.

name: Substantiated Integrity
description: Ascertain whether critical system elements have been corrupted.

name: Integrity Checks
description: Apply and validate checks of the integrity or quality of information, components, or services.

name: Provenance Tracking
description: Identify and track the provenance of data, software, or hardware elements.

name: Behavior Validation
description: Validate the behavior of a system, service, or device against defined or emergent criteria (e.g., requirements, patterns of prior usage).

name: Unpredictability
description: Make changes randomly or unpredictably.

name: Temporal Unpredictability
description: Change behavior or state at times that are determined randomly or by complex functions.

name: Contextual Unpredictability
description: Change behavior or state in ways that are determined randomly or by complex functions.

name: Sensor Fusion and Analysis
description: Fuse and analyze monitoring data and analysis results from different information sources or at different times, together with externally provided threat intelligence.

name: Evolvability
description: Provide mechanisms and structure resources to enable the system to be maintained, modified, extended, or used in new ways without increasing security or mission risk.

name: Forensic and Behavioral Analysis
description: Analyze adversary TTPs, including observed behavior as well as malware and other artifacts left behind by adverse events.

name: Calibrated Defense-in-Depth
description: Provide complementary protective mechanisms at different architectural layers or in different locations, calibrating the strength and number of mechanisms to resource value.

name: Consistency Analysis
description: Determine whether and how protections can be applied in a coordinated, consistent way that minimizes interference, potential cascading failures, or coverage gaps.

name: Orchestration
description: Coordinate the ongoing behavior of mechanisms and processes at different layers, in different locations, or implemented for different aspects of trustworthiness to avoid causing cascading failures, interference, or coverage gaps.

name: Support agility and architect for adaptability
Change or disrupt the attack surface
Make the effects of deception and unpredictability user- transparent
Partition and Ensure Critical Functions at Mission Completion Performance Levels
Baseline and Monitor Systems and Detect Anomalies
Manage System Performance and Enable Cyberspace Defense
Activly Manage System's Configurations to Achieve and Maintain an Operationally-relevant Cyber Risk Posture
Dynamically Restrict Traffic or Isolate Resources
Switch to Alternate System or Component
Minimize Duration of Connection or Session
Isolate or Contain Selected Applications or Components
Monitor Use of Libraries and Utilities
